description.task: Set up your Java development environment and write a Hello World program to confirm everything is installed and configured correctly.
instructions: Install JDK 17 or later and set up an IDE (IntelliJ IDEA, Eclipse, or VS Code with Java extensions).
Create a new Java project and a file named HelloWorld.java.
Add code inside the main() method to print 'Hello, World!' using System.out.println().
Compile and run the program to verify it prints the expected output.
description.hint: If you get 'javac is not recognized', ensure JAVA_HOME is set and the JDK bin folder is added to your system PATH.

description.task: Write a Java program that prints your name, the course you are studying, and today's date — using separate println statements.
instructions: Declare three String variables: name, course, and date.
Assign them your actual name, the course name, and today's date.
Print each in the format: 'Name: John Doe', 'Course: Java Programming', 'Date: 2026-03-19'.
description.hint: String variables are declared as: String variableName = "value";
description.expectedOutput:
Name: John Doe
Course: Java Programming
Date: 2026-03-19

description.task: Write a Java program that uses multiple System.out.println() statements to print a simple pattern or ASCII art of your choice.
description.context: Practising multiple print statements reinforces your understanding of how code is executed line by line.
instructions: Print the diamond pattern exactly as shown in the comments using 5 println() statements.
Each line of the pattern must use the exact spaces and asterisks shown.
Do not use loops — use only separate System.out.println() calls.
description.hint: Count the leading spaces carefully. Line 1 has 2 spaces before *, Line 2 has 1 space before ***, etc.
